recent.statement: A major wildfire will break out in the western United States
recent.evidence: No captured signal concerns this forecast, and a major western U.S. wildfire would certainly have been recorded, yet none appears.

recent.statement: A magnitude 5.0 earthquake will strike near the city of Akhiok, Alaska
recent.evidence: The only signals report a magnitude 5.5 earthquake in the Scotia Sea, with no signal showing any earthquake near Akhiok, Alaska during the closed window.

recent.statement: A M5+ earthquake will strike the southern East Pacific Rise within the next 24 hours.
recent.evidence: No signal reports an earthquake in the southern East Pacific Rise; all events are in the Scotia Sea, Peru, and Mexico.

recent.statement: A magnitude 5.0 earthquake will strike near the city of Khorugh in Tajikistan
recent.evidence: The only signals describe a magnitude 5.5 earthquake in the Scotia Sea, not any earthquake near Khorugh, Tajikistan, so the forecast event is not shown to have occurred within its closed window.

recent.statement: A magnitude 5.0 earthquake will occur in the Philippines
recent.evidence: The only signals report a 5.5M earthquake in the Scotia Sea, not in the Philippines, so no signal shows the forecast event occurred.

recent.statement: A magnitude 5.0 earthquake will strike near the city of Yakutat, Alaska
recent.evidence: The only signal describes a magnitude 5.5 earthquake in the Scotia Sea, not a magnitude 5.0 quake near Yakutat, Alaska, so no signal shows the forecast event occurred.

recent.statement: A magnitude 5.0 earthquake will strike within the next 24 hours near Puerto Madero, Mexico
recent.evidence: The only nearby earthquakes were M4.4 and M4.2 near Puerto Madero, neither meeting the M5.0 threshold, and the M5.5 event was in the distant Scotia Sea.

recent.statement: A magnitude 4.5 earthquake will strike near Puerto Madero, Mexico
recent.evidence: Signals show several Mexico earthquakes but none is identified as occurring near Puerto Madero, so the specific forecast location is not confirmed.

recent.statement: A 4.5 magnitude earthquake will occur in the next 24 hours
recent.evidence: S2 reports a magnitude 5.5 earthquake in Peru on 07/19/2026, occurring within the window and exceeding the 4.5 magnitude threshold.

recent.statement: A magnitude 5.0 earthquake will occur in the southern Kermadec Islands.
recent.evidence: Both signals report a Scotia Sea earthquake, not the forecasted magnitude 5.0 event in the southern Kermadec Islands, and no signal shows a Kermadec quake within the window.

recent.statement: A magnitude 5.0 earthquake will strike near Puerto Madero, Mexico
recent.evidence: Several Mexico earthquakes are reported (S3, S4, S5) but none names Puerto Madero or otherwise confirms an event at that specific location, so the forecast's stated place is not met.

recent.statement: A magnitude 4.9 earthquake will strike near the coast of Mexico.
recent.evidence: No signal shows a magnitude 4.9 earthquake near the Mexican coast; the Mexico signals report different magnitudes (5.7, 6, 7.3) without specifying a coastal location matching the forecast.
